name: Accounting Society
summary: Accounting Society is a club for all majors who want to see what accounting and business in general is all about. We meet weekly during common hour at 1:00-2:15 in CV Starr 205 with Beta Alpha Psi and Tax Society.

name: Economics Student Association
summary: The Hofstra Economics Club shall seek to unite like-minded curious students and faculty in an effort to explore, learn and hopefully enjoy a complex but rewarding study.

name: Music and Entertainment Industry Student Association
summary: MEISA, short Music and Entertainment Industry Student Association, is a club dedicated to everything in the music. We promote music, artists, and events. We strive to help our members find internships that will lead to careers in the music industry.

name: Black Student Union
summary: BSU (formally known as African Peoples Organization) has been in existence for over 35 years. Born in the 1970s, BSU served as an conglomerate of all minority organizations whose purpose was to attend to the needs of the minority population at Hofstra.

name: Zarb Asian Pacific Students Association
summary: (Graduate Students Only) We are a Zarb sponsored non-profit student organization to develop and foster relationships among Asian Pacific students. Formerly known as the Zarb Asian Student Association.

name: Hofstra Filmmakers' Club
summary: The Hofstra Filmmakers' Club (HFC) is a student-run club open to all students who share a love and passion for cinema.

name: Masquerade Musical Theatre Co.
summary: Masquerade Musical Theatre Company is dedicated to providing the Hofstra community with a creative outlet in order to explore all aspects of Musical Theatre.

name: The Hofbeats
summary: One of Hofstra's award-winning, co-ed a cappella groups!

name: Hofstra University Democrats
summary: The Hofstra University Democrats are committed to creating an inclusive and welcoming environment where members can engage in thoughtful and respectful discussions while learning about the democratic political process and ideals.

name: College Republicans
summary: An inviting forum to discuss current events and act upon the ideals that we as Americans believe in.

name: Hofstra American Marketing Association
summary: The Hofstra American Marketing Association (HAMA) is one of the pre-professional organizations at Hofstra University. HAMA takes pride in maintaining a welcoming environment with abundant opportunities for the next generation of business professionals.

name: Phi Alpha Delta
summary: The purpose of this Fraternity shall be to form a strong bond uniting students and teachers of the law with members of the Bench and Bar in a fraternal fellowship designed to advance the ideals of liberty and equal justice under law.

name: Hofstra Tax Society
summary: The Tax Society provides networking and tax experience to members.  Chiefly, we organize the VITA/TCE program, where members volunteer to prepare actual tax returns for low-income individuals.

name: Student Government Association
summary: The Student Government Association serves as the governing body for all clubs and organizations.  It is also the representative organization for the student voice on the campus of Hofstra University.

name: Residence Hall Association
summary: RHA's mission is to serve as a voice for the residential students of Hofstra University and to enhance the educational, social, recreational, and cultural aspects of residence life while providing leadership training within the residence halls.

name: Beta Alpha Psi
summary: Beta Alpha Psi is an honor organization for financial information students and professionals. At Hofstra University, the Delta Pi chapter is focused on promoting the international goals of BAP through our professional meetings and exciting events.

name: Zarb Women in Business
summary: (Graduate Students Only) Zarb Women in Business is a professional association at Hofstra University that strives to promote the empowerment of women and women's presence in all levels and industries in business.

name: Alpha Phi
summary:
Alpha Phi is a sisterhood of outstanding women supporting one another in lifelong achievement.
The sisterhood of Alpha Phi stretches from coast to coast through 173 collegiate campuses and more than 200,000 members.

name: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity Inc.
summary: Alpha Phi Alpha Fraternity Inc., develops leaders, promotes brotherhood and academic excellence, while providing advocacy and service for our community.

name: Hofstra Athletic Training Society
summary: HATS is a pre-professional graduate student organization for those interested in the profession of athletic training. HATS participates in a variety of community service, educational and social events annually.

name: Hofstra Graduate Marketing Association
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The primary objective of the Marketing Association is to assist all members with the continuing challenges faced in marketing careers. In addition, this organization helps Zarb business students by providing them with oppurtuniti

name: PhD in Applied Organizational Psychology Student Organization
summary: (Graduate Students Only) An organization for PhD students in the Applied Organizational Psychology program at Hofstra.

name: Zarb International MBA/MS Association
summary: (Graduate Students Only) ZIMA Culture in its broadest sense is cultivated behavior, that is the totality of a person's learned, accumulated experience which is socially transmitted, more precisely; behavior through social learning.

name: Zarb Black & Hispanic M.B.A./M.S. Association
summary:
(Graduate Students Only)
As a diverse group, we aspire to promote and maintain a positive network among our students, alumni, faculty and administrators.

name: Higher Education Graduate Student Association
summary: (Graduate Students Only) HEGSA is an organization of graduate students currently pursuing careers in the field of Higher Education.

name: Phi Eta Sigma
summary: "Knowledge is power." Phi Eta Sigma is a national honors society that recognizes the academic excellence of Freshman students who achieve a minimum GPA of 3.5 during their first or second semester.

name: Chinese Students and Scholars Association
summary: (Graduate Students Only) Our association is aiming to help Chinese students to know Hofstra, to understand American culture, and to get involved in American social life.

name: Hofstra Business Consulting Group
summary: Since its origins in 1988, the Hofstra Business Consulting Group has grown to become a powerful source of strategic consulting for growing businesses in the New York metropolitan area. HBCG consultants are M.B.A. and J.D. / M.B.A. candidates

name: National Student Speech Language Hearing Association
summary: The National Student Speech-Language Hearing Association chapter at Hofstra University provides students a chance to get involved on campus and in the local community through volunteer opportunities and participation in fundraising events.

name: United States Institute for Theater Technology
summary: Hofstra USITT upholds the mission of the National USITT group.

name: Delta Gamma
summary: Built on a foundation of fostering high ideals of friendship, promoting educational and cultural interests, a true sense of social responsibility and developing the best qualities of character, Delta Gamma Fraternity a remarkable organization.

name: Public Relations Student Society of America
summary: Hofstra's PRSSA is an organization designed to help students enhance their knowledge of public relations and provide access to professional development opportunities through networking events, workshops, internships, guest speakers, and conferences.

name: Student Members of the American Chemical Society
summary: A service, social, and professional development club for students interested in chemistry.

name: The Hofstra University Dutchmen
summary: Founded in 2011, The Dutchmen are Hofstra's only all-male singing group. We work hard to provide fun/engaging performances, and have a great time doing it! To learn more about what we do, look us up on all social media platforms @HofstraDutchmen.

name: American Choral Directors Association
summary: ACDA is a group of future Music Educators coming together to explore the world of choral directing through professional development through speakers, conferences, and outreach programs.

name: Industrial/Organizational Psychology Student Association
summary:
(Graduate Students Only) 

IOPSA is an organization for I/O Psychology Students. Our goal is to foster a collaborative environment, provide networking and career building opportunities, and much more.

name: Graduate Students in Clinical Psychology
summary: (Graduate Students Only) Clinical psychology doctoral students.

name: Corporate Social Responsibility Association
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The mission of the Zarb CSRA is to enhance Zarb graduate business students’ knowledge and experience with socially conscious business practices through various types of activities.

name: Beta Beta Beta Biological Honor Society
summary: TriBeta is an honor society for students majoring in the biological sciences. These students are dedicated to improving the understanding and appreciation of biological study and extending boundaries of human knowledge through scientific research.

name: Educational Leadership Doctoral Fellows
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The Educational Leadership Doctoral Fellows is a student organization for doctoral students in all phases of the Ed.D. program in Educational and Policy Leadership.

name: Future Healthcare Leaders of Hofstra University
summary: (Graduate Students Only) The Future Healthcare Leaders of Hofstra University works to ensure opportunities for knowledge sharing and career development among future healthcare management professionals within our organization and local community.

name: Society of Physics Students
summary: Hofstra's chapter of the Society of Physics Students, is a group of multicultural Physics-lovers (of many different majors) working together to advocate for Physics education and appreciation among the Hofstra community.

name: Special Education Graduate Association
summary: (Graduate Students Only) A graduate student organization that provides opportunities for members to participate in activities related to the field of special education, as well as attend various learning opportunities.

name: American String Teachers Association
summary: As a collegiate chapter of the American String Teachers Association (ASTA), we focus on string teacher education through professional speakers and workshops, providing networking opportunities, and offering volunteer work.

name: Physician Assistant Club
summary: To educate and inform students about the Physician Assistant Career and to provide volunteer services to the local community and students on campus. Club is open to all Hofstra Students interested in the Physician Assistant Career.

name: Zeta Phi Eta
summary: The Hofstra Chapter of Zeta Phi Eta, a national pre-professional fraternity works to develop meaningful and lasting professional relationships and connections within the Lawrence Herbert School of Communication.

name: Student Computing Services
summary: Student Computing Services assists students with software questions and training.

name: Alpha Phi Omega
summary: Alpha Phi Omega (ΑΦΩ or APO) is a gender-inclusive, international community service organization. Our founding principles are Leadership, Friendship and Service.

name: SheEOs
summary: Women in business undergraduate organization that helps students build networks, learn new business concepts, and develop themselves professionally.

name: American Society of Mechanical Engineers
summary: Mechanical Engineering Club with a focus on hands-on projects involving mechanical, aeronautics, and electrical components. All majors welcome.

name: Center for Academic Excellence
summary: The Center for Academic Excellence (CAE) is dedicated to help students achieve success in their academics and overall college experience. The CAE promotes improved academic performance and student persistence.

name: American Cancer Society on Campus at Hofstra University
summary: The American Cancer Society (ACS) at Hofstra University is a student-run organization dedicated to raising awareness, fundraising, and supporting those impacted by cancer through education, advocacy, and community service.

name: Student Veterans Association
summary: The Hofstra SVA aims to support and advocate on behalf of student veterans/service members on campus, as they transition into their academic and professional careers. We are a fully inclusive organization, and welcome all Hofstra students and faculty.

name: Hofstra Tabletop Gamers Club
summary: HofTop provides a safe space to play board games, card games, RPGs, etc. and make new friends. With a combined collection of 127 different board games and counting, we believe we have a game for every like and interest. Beginners and experts welcome!

name: Business Professionals of America (BPA)
summary: BPA is a business competition club where we learn, network, and attend events that will strengthen our core understanding and lay the foundation for the next steps in our lives.

name: Society of Women Engineers at Hofstra
summary: This organization supports women engineers and computer scientists on campus. It will be the goal of the Society of Women Engineers club to empower all Hofstra students to succeed and advance in science and engineering.

name: Title IX Office for Student Issues
summary: This department is used for tracking Title IX Training for Student Organizations

name: Student Employment
summary: Communication tool for Office of Student Employment

name: Queer and Trans People of Color Coalition
summary: The Queer & Trans People of Color Coalition strives to create a safe space conducive for LGBTQ+ people of color and their allies to have proactive discussions about social justice, effective allyship & how to evoke positive change in our communities.

name: First Generation Pride
summary: First Generation students are defined as students where neither of their parents has completed a 4 year college degree in the United States.

name: Hofstra Math Club
summary: The Hofstra Math Club is here to unify students in various area of STEM under a common foundation: math! Our members come from a plethora of disciplines, and we are here to help them develop friendships through mathematics.

name: Hofstra Neuroscience Club
summary: Hofstra Neuroscience Club is designed to educate, enlighten, and help the community. We abide by our core standards – known as the three C’s – which are the following: Communication, Collaboration, and Creativity.

name: Professional Fraternity Council
summary: PFC promotes and enhances professional fraternities at Hofstra by serving as a cooperative forum for sharing ideas and information, increasing the awareness of professional fraternities; and creating a spirit of fellowship among students.

name: Biology Club
summary: The Biology Club is dedicated to connecting students with an interest in Biology, through community service, leadership, mentorship, education, and developing relationships between students and faculty.

name: Hofstra Hedge Fund Association
summary: Our mission is to cultivate a diverse business community at Hofstra University by empowering students to achieve their academic and professional goals through discussions of current events and various educational and career building themes.

name: Phi Beta Kappa
summary: Phi Beta Kappa, the nation's oldest honor society, was founded in 1776. Hofstra University joined the community of this organization in 1973, after professors were granted a charter to form the Omega of New York Chapter.

name: Project Sunshine
summary: Project Sunshine is a club open to anyone in any year and any major. It gives Hofstra students the chance to give back to the community and to create fun and memorable moments with the children in hospitals.

name: New York State Band Directors Association
summary: The New York State Band Directors Association Collegiate Chapter is a not-for-profit collegiate organization composed of future Band Directors and Teachers. It is founded for the purpose of improving and promoting instrumental music.

name: AnimateHofstra
summary: The purpose of this organization is to cultivate animation at Hofstra. We seek to incorporate aspects of animation culture and industry at our meetings. The practice of production and technique will be emphasized through discussion and workshops.

name: Hofstra International Business Association
summary: The Hofstra International Business Association provides a cooperative and stimulating environment that unites members of the Hofstra community who share an interest in international business and want to have intercultural experiences.

name: Women in Cybersecurity
summary: Our goal is to provide students who are interested in the field of cybersecurity a welcoming and encouraging environment to learn, share ideas, meet new people, and empower all students to succeed in computer science and cybersecurity-related fields.

name: Zarb Technology Club
summary: Welcome to the Z.T.C where we expose students to current / future technologies in business, and create an environment where students can collaborate on projects related to cybersecurity and technology in the Zarb School of Business.

name: Zarb Dean's Business Scholars
summary: The official GIHU page of Zarb Dean's Business Scholars

name: Future Educators at Hofstra
summary: An organization geared towards preparing future educators.

name: Pre-Law (Advising)
summary: Pre-Law advising through the Center for University Advising

name: Hofstra Nari
summary: This organization is to provide a safe haven for South Asian women on campus. The South Asian community needs to amplify the voices of South Asian women. Our main goals of the club are empowerment, awareness, community, intersectionality and diversity.

name: Disability Rights Education Activism and Mentoring - Hofstra
summary: DREAM Hofstra advocates for student rights, increased accessibility, social and policy changes, and aims to provide support and mentorship for disabled students

name: Hofstra Comics and Manga Club
summary: The Hofstra Comics and Manga Club (H.C.M.C) was founded to teach and entertain students in the creation of comic book and manga characters, and to also discuss their widespread influence in media within movies, television shows, and popular culture.

name: Office of Commuting Student Services and Community Outreach
summary: Office of Commuting Student Services and Community Outreach

name: Zarb School of Business
summary: Official event page of the Frank G. Zarb School of Business

name: International Enrollment
summary: Serving Hofstra's F-1 and J-1 international students and scholars, International Enrollment provides a variety of programs and immigration services to meet the unique needs of our international community.

name: Community Engagement and Outreach
summary: Community Engagement and Outreach

name: ActiveMinds at Hofstra University
summary: Active Minds is a student-led organization that is dedicated to breaking down the stigma that surrounds mental health!

name: Hofstra C.O.D.E
summary: Hofstra C.O.D.E aims to be a general computer science club for people who are interested in learning about computers and for people looking to expand their knowledge in computers.

name: Project EmpowHer
summary: Project EmpowHer is a club that aims to advocate for underserved communities of women such as those who are homeless, domestic abuse victims, refugees etc.

name: Women in Film Hofstra
summary: Women in Film Hofstra is for all students interested in learning more about women filmmakers. We aim to foster an inclusive environment through education and empowerment, highlighting women’s place in the film industry.

name: Alpha Psi Omega
summary: National Theatre Honor Society - Our aim is to encourage dramatic production at every step in a person’s academic career.

name: Office of Study Abroad Programs
summary: Your Hofstra journey can be a global journey by participating in a study abroad program. The Office of Study Abroad Programs provides Hofstra students with these global opportunities.

name: Office of Equity and Inclusion
summary: The Office of Equity and Inclusion endorses a variety of campus-wide events and programs throughout the year, all aimed at celebrating the diverse community and promoting multicultural understanding.

name: The National Society of Leadership and Success
summary: We are a collaborative network of burgeoning or established leaders dedicated to the spread and development of leadership. Through effective external programming, combined with in-depth group discourse, the NSLS creates the next generation of leaders.

name: For the CULTURE
summary: For the CULTURE was created by and for artists and music business professionals of color within the Hofstra community to provide an inclusive space to network, collaborate, and amplify their voices.

name: HU Clinical Psychology PHD Equity and Social Justice Initiative
summary: ESJI is dedicated to fostering an inclusive academic and social environment at Hofstra University by addressing and advocating for diversity, equity, and inclusion (DEI).

name: Honors Board
summary: To oversee ongoing efforts to maintain the visibility and effectiveness of Hofstra's Honor Code via marketing campaigns, websites, and other promotional activities.

name: First-Gen Starting Line
summary: First-Gen Starting Line builds community for all Hofstra students through connection, mentorship, and support—empowering students to grow, thrive, and lead together from the very start of their college journey.

name: Engineers in Action
summary: Engineers in Action connects students with communities worldwide to design and build sustainable infrastructure, like pedestrian bridges, that improve access to schools, healthcare, and opportunity while giving members hands-on engineering experience.

name: Planned Parenthood Generation Action at Hofstra
summary: PPGA at Hofstra advances sexual health and reproductive rights through education, advocacy, and service. With PPGNY, we host trainings, volunteer work, and civic engagement centered in intersectional equity, access, & leadership. Open to all students!

name: Institute of Innovation and Entrepreneurship/ideaHUb
summary: Hofstra University’s Institute of Innovation and Entrepreneurship (IIE) is a hub for bold ideas, collaboration, and real-world impact.

name: NOAH Scholars
summary: The New Opportunities at Hofstra Program (NOAH) has provided highly motivated and diverse students with access to higher education at one of the country’s finest postsecondary institutions since the program’s inception in 1964.
